summary: Path traversal guard uses string prefix check that is bypassable, readFile error handling is inconsistent between top-level and subdirectory reads, and one test is redundant with another.

suggestions.description: startsWith prefix check is bypassable for sibling directories
suggestions.rationale: If cwd is /home/user, the path /home/user-evil/payload resolves and passes startsWith("/home/user") because it shares the same string prefix. This is a real path traversal bypass when directory names share a common prefix.
suggestions.suggestion: Change to: if (resolved \!== cwd && \!resolved.startsWith(cwd + path.sep)) to ensure the match is on a full path segment boundary.

suggestions.description: Top-level readFile has no error handling, but subdirectory readFile does
suggestions.rationale: Line 47 uses bare readFile with no .catch(), so a permission error or race condition on a top-level .md file will throw an unhandled error and abort the entire operation. Meanwhile readSubDir on line 69 uses .catch(() => null) and gracefully skips unreadable files. This inconsistency means the function is less resilient for top-level files than subdirectory files.
suggestions.suggestion: Add .catch(() => null) to the top-level readFile call on line 47, and filter out nulls, matching the pattern already used in readSubDir.

suggestions.description: Test "accepts relative paths within cwd" is redundant with "reads .md files from top-level directory"
suggestions.rationale: Both tests create .notickets/epic.md as a relative path within cwd and call readNoTicketsDir(".notickets"). The beforeEach already chdir-s into a temp directory, so every test is already using relative paths within cwd. This test exercises no unique code path.
suggestions.suggestion: Either remove this test or make it meaningfully different, e.g., test an absolute path within cwd to verify the validation accepts it.

suggestions.description: No test for the startsWith prefix bypass
suggestions.rationale: The security-critical path validation is only tested with /etc and ../etc traversal. There is no test for the sibling-directory attack vector (e.g., a path that shares a string prefix with cwd but is a different directory). This is the exact bug present in the implementation.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test that creates a sibling directory to cwd (e.g., testDir + "-evil") and asserts that readNoTicketsDir rejects it.

suggestions.description: Intermediate entries array with empty content is unnecessarily complex
suggestions.rationale: Lines 38-39 push FileEntry objects with content: "" into the entries array, then lines 45-49 map over them to read the actual content. This two-pass approach creates throwaway objects. The old sequential implementation was more straightforward.
suggestions.suggestion: Collect just the paths of top-level .md files (string[]), then Promise.all the reads directly, avoiding the intermediate FileEntry[] with placeholder content.
